input.command:
ADMIN_PASS=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
REPORTING_PASS=$(kubectl get secret reporting-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
echo "Admin pass: $ADMIN_PASS"
echo "Reporting pass: $REPORTING_PASS"

input.command:
# Check for admin user - maybe username is different
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval "db.adminCommand({listDatabases: 1})" 2>&1 | head -20

# Also try with mongo binary
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- bash -c "mongosh --version" 2>&1

  --eval "db.getUsers()" 2>&1

content.thinking: The logs show that the admin user is `admin-user`, not `admin`. Let me connect with `admin-user`.

content.text: The admin username is `admin-user`. Let me connect with the correct credentials.

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval "rs.status().members.map(m => ({name: m.name, state: m.stateStr}))" 2>&1
input.description: Check replica set status with correct admin-user credentials

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval "
db.adminCommand({listDatabases: 1}).databases.map(d => d.name)
" 2>&1

kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval "
db.system.users.find({}, {user: 1, roles: 1, _id: 0}).toArray()
" 2>&1
